title: Public Advisory- False or Misleading Claims re: “Mohammed Ali”
text:
PUBLIC ADVISORY
REGARDING FALSE OR MISLEADING CLAIMS BY AN INDIVIDUAL
OPERATING AS “MOHAMMED ALI”
The Trinidad and Tobago Securities and Exchange Commission (“TTSEC”) is warning the public of
the unauthorized use of its name by an individual operating under the name “Mohammed Ali”,
who is also known to use the following online identifiers in advertisements on social media and
messaging platforms such as Telegram: @mohammed_profits, @mohammedprofits, and
@mohammed_tobago. This warning is issued to protect the public.
False and Misleading Representations
Staff of the TTSEC have become aware of a document being disseminated online purporting to be
a licence or certificate issued to “Mohmmed Ali” which falsely claims to be associated with the
TTSEC and its official address. In particular, the document:
• Identifies the individual by name as the applicant;
• Bears the header, Trinidad and Tobago License, International Financial Services
Commission;
• States that the individual is “of the Trinidad and Tobago Securities and Exchange
Commission”;
• States that the individual has been granted a license for international asset protection
and management; and
• References the TTSEC’s official address at Level 22–23, Tower D, 1 Wrightson Road, Port
of Spain.
The TTSEC wishes to make it unequivocally clear that:
• It has no association or relationship with this individual or the activities being
conducted;
• It has not issued any licence, certificate, approval or authorisation to any individual
operating under the names “Mohammed Ali” and
• Any document suggesting that the individual is affiliated with, licensed by, employed
by, or operating from the TTSEC is false and misleading.
Not Registered with the TTSEC
The TTSEC further advises that “Mohammed Ali” is not registered with the TTSEC in any
capacity and is not authorised under the Securities Act, Chapter 83:02 to:
• Offer securities or investment products;
• Provide investment advice;
• Manage or trade investments on behalf of clients;
• Solicit funds from members of the public for investment purposes;
• Conduct any other activity regulated under the Securities Act; or
• Use its name and address in any document suggesting he/she is otherwise associated
with TTSEC.
Any such claims should be regarded as false and misleading.

Public Warning
The TTSEC urges members of the public to exercise extreme caution, refrain from investing in
suspicious investment opportunities and to be particularly wary of any investment opportunity
that:
• Claims an affiliation with the TTSEC or any other regulator without independent
verification;
• Promises unusually high returns, especially within hours or days;
• Requires additional payments such as “taxes” or “fees” to access profits;
• Requests that funds be transferred to third-party or personal bank accounts; or
• Is promoted through social media messaging platforms such as Telegram.
Important Reminder
The TTSEC reminds the public to always:
• Verify that any person or entity offering investment opportunities is registered with the
TTSEC;
• Conduct independent due diligence before investing; and
• Be cautious of unsolicited online investment offers.
A list of registered entities and individuals authorised under the Securities Act is available on the
TTSEC’s website at www.ttsec.org.tt.
Stay alert. Stay safe. Protect your money.
Dated: January 27, 2026
